zones.label: Band Positions
zones.summary: The four colored bands on a resistor, read left to right
facts: Band 1 = 1st Digit
Band 2 = 2nd Digit
Band 3 = Multiplier (how many zeros, or the power of ten to multiply by)
Band 4 = Tolerance (how far the real value can be off)
Always read the bands from left to right, starting at the end with the bands grouped closest together

zones.label: Worked Example
zones.summary: Reading Brown · Black · Red · Gold step by step
facts: Brown = 1 (1st digit)
Black = 0 (2nd digit)
Red = ×100 Ω (multiplier)
Gold = ±5% (tolerance)

zones.label: Color Value Table
zones.summary: Look up each color's digit, multiplier, and tolerance
facts: Orange 3 · ×1 kΩ · — ;  Yellow 4 · ×10 kΩ · — ;  Green 5 · ×100 kΩ · ±0.5%
White 9 · ×1 GΩ · —
Gold and Silver have no digit: Gold ×0.1 Ω · ±5% ;  Silver ×0.01 Ω · ±10%
Columns: Color Swatch · Color Name · Digit (Bands 1-2) · Multiplier (Band 3) · Tolerance (Band 4)

zones.label: Mnemonic
zones.summary: A memory phrase for the digit order 0 to 9
facts: "Bad Beer Rots Our Young Guts But Vodka Goes Well"
The first letter of each word matches a color in order
Black · Brown · Red · Orange · Yellow · Green · Blue · Violet · Gray · White
These map to digits 0 through 9

zones.label: Color Code Standard
zones.summary: The official standard these color codes come from
facts: Color code standard: IEC 60062
Source: IEC 60062:2016 — Marking codes for resistors and capacitors
IEC stands for the International Electrotechnical Commission
The same standard is used by resistor makers worldwide

quiz.question: On a 4-band resistor, which band tells you the multiplier (how many zeros to add)?
quiz.explanation: Band 3 is the multiplier. Bands 1 and 2 are the first two digits, and Band 4 is the tolerance.

quiz.question: What digit does the color Green stand for?
quiz.explanation: In the color value table, Green = 5. Its multiplier is ×100 kΩ and its tolerance is ±0.5%.

quiz.question: A resistor has bands Brown · Black · Red · Gold. What is its value?
Which phrase helps you remember the color order for digits 0 through 9?
quiz.explanation: "Bad Beer Rots Our Young Guts But Vodka Goes Well" — each word's first letter matches Black, Brown, Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, Violet, Gray, White.
